properties: Uragoga tenuis, also known as the African cherry or uda tree, has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. Uragoga tenuis is also said to have antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties, which may help manage blood sugar levels and protect against oxidative stress.
